name: Redbrook to Penallt (Wye Valley)
terrain: Woodland, riverside, steep climb to hilltop church
highlights: Lovely loop over the old railway bridge linking England and Wales.
points_of_interest: Disused Monmouth–Chepstow railway viaduct; Penallt Old Church (Norman); former tinplate works
viewpoints: Wye Valley from Penallt; riverside meadows
picnic_spots: Penallt village green; riverside below viaduct
parking_start: Redbrook village layby / Forge Side parking
food_drink_nearby: The Boat Inn Redbrook (by the river, dog friendly); The Bush Inn Penallt
hazards_notes: Steep climb to Penallt; wooden bridge is pedestrian only
